commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.fix: Add hover states to the forecast stack elements to improve user feedback.

issues.description: The layout is functional but could benefit from subtle background differences or distinct spacing for the forecast strip to separate it from the current weather data.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

issues.description: The requirement for a 'Final review step' was missed. Currently, the form jumps from Step 4 (Comments) directly to submission.
issues.fix: Add a 5th step that displays all gathered data in a read-only list/table before calling handleSubmit.

issues.description: The Progress component lacks an aria-valuenow or aria-label for screen readers.
issues.fix: Add aria-label={`Step ${step} of 4`} to the Progress component.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, clean, and follows the design system patterns effectively. It is highly functional, but the omission of the requested 'Final review' step prevents it from meeting the full requirements of the prompt.

issues.description: The logic for the progress bar calculation is slightly off, labeling it 'Step 1 of 5' instead of 'Step 1 of 4' as implied by the 4-step survey requirement (review is the 5th step).
issues.fix: Adjust the step logic to explicitly label and treat the Review page as part of the flow correctly or ensure the prompt requirements (4 steps + review) are distinct.

issues.description: Lack of visual feedback for validation states when the user has touched the fields (e.g., fields aren't marked as 'invalid' visually until focused/blurred).
issues.fix: Add 'touched' state tracking to inputs so validation errors only appear after the user has interacted with the field.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured and functional, cleanly separating the state management logic from the UI. It adheres well to the design system primitives, though the step count logic needs a slight refinement to align perfectly with the user's mental model of '4 steps' plus a final review.

issues.fix: Adjust the step logic to explicitly label and treat the Review page as part of the flow correctly or ensure the prompt requirements (4 steps + review) are distinct.

issues.fix: Add 'touched' state tracking to inputs so validation errors only appear after the user has interacted with the field.

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

issues.description: The requirement for double-click editing of task titles is missing.
issues.fix: Add an 'isEditing' state per card and replace the Title Text with an Input field on double-click.

issues.description: The 'Move' dropdown implementation is slightly clunky, using a component designed for selection as a trigger-based action.
issues.fix: Use a custom popover or a more explicit action menu to ensure the move operation is intuitive.

issues.description: Lack of ARIA labels on dynamic elements like the 'Move' button and 'Add Task' buttons.
issues.fix: Add aria-label attributes to icon-only buttons and interactive cards to ensure screen reader support.

issues.description: The layout lacks a clear visual distinction between columns and the background, making the board look flat.
issues.fix: Add a subtle border or specific background color to the column containers to better define the Kanban lanes.

evaluations.critique: The component is functional and well-structured, successfully implementing the core Kanban requirements. It handles state effectively but lacks some of the requested 'polishing' features like inline editing and enhanced accessibility markers.

issues.description: The requirements stated to display assignee initials and due date, but these fields are ignored in the task card UI.
issues.fix: Add Avatar/initials and date display to the Card component inside the map loop.

issues.description: Task editing feature (double-click or edit button) is missing from the requirement description.
issues.fix: Add an edit state or icon button to the Task Card.

issues.description: Inputs in the 'Add Task' form lack associated labels for screen readers or are handled via labels that might not be correctly linked.
issues.fix: Ensure FormField components or aria-labels are explicitly used to associate inputs.

issues.description: The layout uses horizontal scrolling for columns, which works, but the 'Add Task' button at the bottom of each column is not strictly aligned to the bottom if column heights differ significantly.
issues.fix: Use flex-grow on the task list container to push the 'Add Task' button to the bottom consistently.

evaluations.critique: The component provides a functional and clean Kanban board that correctly uses design tokens and state management. To reach production quality, the UI should be updated to include the requested metadata (assignee/date) and the missing editing functionality.

issues.description: The requirements stated to display assignee initials and due date, but these fields are ignored in the task card UI.
issues.fix: Add Avatar/initials and date display to the Card component inside the map loop.

issues.description: Task editing feature (double-click or edit button) is missing from the requirement description.
issues.fix: Add an edit state or icon button to the Task Card.

issues.description: Inputs in the 'Add Task' form lack associated labels for screen readers or are handled via labels that might not be correctly linked.
issues.fix: Ensure FormField components or aria-labels are explicitly used to associate inputs.

issues.description: The layout uses horizontal scrolling for columns, which works, but the 'Add Task' button at the bottom of each column is not strictly aligned to the bottom if column heights differ significantly.
issues.fix: Use flex-grow on the task list container to push the 'Add Task' button to the bottom consistently.

evaluation.critique: The component provides a functional and clean Kanban board that correctly uses design tokens and state management. To reach production quality, the UI should be updated to include the requested metadata (assignee/date) and the missing editing functionality.

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Legend component is missing, which was explicitly requested to show categories.
issues.fix: Map over the 'categories' array and render a small legend below the grid showing colors/category names.

issues.description: Elements are not color-coded by category as requested. Currently, all elements use a default card style.
issues.fix: Create a mapping function or object to assign specific background/border colors (using design tokens) based on the category string.

issues.description: The interactive table cells are standard div elements, not accessible buttons.
issues.fix: Change div to button or add role='button' and tabIndex={0}, and implement onKeyDown handler for accessibility.

issues.description: Missing hover states on grid elements to indicate interactability.
issues.fix: Add a CSS hover state to the grid items that changes the background color or shadow.

evaluations.critique: The component structure is solid and logic for searching is correct. It needs to address visual hierarchy by implementing the requested category color-coding and adding the missing legend to make the table fully functional and user-friendly.

issues.description: Grid cells have fixed styling that may cause clipping or overlap if container width is small, and hover states are not visually distinct.
issues.fix: Add a :hover scale transform or border highlight to the element cards to provide immediate visual feedback.

issues.description: The transition state for filtering is immediate, which can be jarring.
issues.fix: Apply a CSS transition to the opacity property of the buttons for a smoother filtering experience.

evaluations.critique: The component is highly functional and uses the grid layout effectively to represent the periodic table. The code is clean and modular. To reach a production-ready score, I would recommend adding interactive feedback (hover/focus states) and perhaps a smooth transition on the filtering animation.

issues.fix: Apply a CSS transition to the opacity property of the buttons for a smoother filtering experience.

evaluation.critique: The component is highly functional and uses the grid layout effectively to represent the periodic table. The code is clean and modular. commit.name: E-Commerce Product Page
commit.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
commit.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

product.description: Product object with id, name, price, currency, rating, stock, description, specifications[], reviews[]

example.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
example.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

addToCart.label: Add to Cart
addToCart.description: Called when user clicks Add to Cart. Payload: { productId, quantity }

product.name: Wireless Noise-Cancelling Headphones
product.description: Premium wireless headphones with adaptive noise cancellation, 30-hour battery life, and ultra-comfortable memory foam ear cushions.

reviews.author: Alex M.
reviews.text: Best headphones I have ever owned. The noise cancellation is incredible.

reviews.author: Sarah K.
reviews.text: Great sound quality. A bit tight for my head but breaks in after a week.

reviews.author: James P.
reviews.text: Battery life is exactly as advertised. Very impressed.

issues.description: Missing content for Specifications and Reviews tabs; current implementation just shows placeholder text.
issues.fix: Map through props.product.specifications and props.product.reviews to render actual data.

issues.description: Rating stars are missing entirely from the UI.
issues.fix: Implement a loop or component that renders star icons based on the props.product.rating value.

issues.description: Layout responsiveness is missing; the Stack direction does not switch to vertical on small screens.
issues.fix: Apply responsive breakpoints to the main Stack direction (e.g., direction={{ base: 'vertical', md: 'horizontal' }}).

issues.description: Quantity selector is purely text-based; lacks distinct UI controls or layout.
issues.fix: Use input/button components consistently and improve spacing for the quantity control area.

evaluations.critique: The component structure is clean and follows design system principles, but it is fundamentally incomplete as it fails to render the requested data for the specifications and reviews tabs. Adding the missing dynamic lists and rating visualizer would significantly improve the product detail experience.

issues.description: The image placeholder is overly large and lacks a responsive container adjustment, which may lead to awkward aspect ratios on mobile.
issues.fix: Use a flexible aspect-ratio container or constrain the height of the image placeholder box.

issues.description: The quantity selector buttons are missing aria-labels, and the star icons are purely visual without screen-reader text explaining the rating.
issues.fix: Add aria-label='Increment/Decrement quantity' to buttons and aria-label='{product.rating} out of 5 stars' to the star container.

issues.description: The star icons are rendered using the same 'star' icon name regardless of whether they are filled or empty, relying only on color change.
issues.fix: Use conditional icon names (e.g., 'star-filled' vs 'star-outline') if the library supports it, or ensure visual distinction via contrast.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, implements all functional requirements, and follows the design system patterns effectively. To reach production-ready status, it primarily needs better accessibility labels for interactive elements and slight layout refinements for responsive mobile viewing.

issues.description: The image placeholder is overly large and lacks a responsive container adjustment, which may lead to awkward aspect ratios on mobile.
issues.fix: Use a flexible aspect-ratio container or constrain the height of the image placeholder box.

issues.description: The quantity selector buttons are missing aria-labels, and the star icons are purely visual without screen-reader text explaining the rating.
issues.fix: Add aria-label='Increment/Decrement quantity' to buttons and aria-label='{product.rating} out of 5 stars' to the star container.

issues.description: The star icons are rendered using the same 'star' icon name regardless of whether they are filled or empty, relying only on color change.
issues.fix: Use conditional icon names (e.g., 'star-filled' vs 'star-outline') if the library supports it, or ensure visual distinction via contrast.

evaluation.critique: The component is well-structured, implements all functional requirements, and follows the design system patterns effectively. To reach production-ready status, it primarily needs better accessibility labels for interactive elements and slight layout refinements for responsive mobile viewing.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

example.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

issues.description: The requirement to 'group consecutive messages from same sender' was not implemented.
issues.fix: Add logic to check if message[i].sender === message[i-1].sender and hide the avatar/name metadata for consecutive messages.

issues.description: Hardcoded colors (e.g., #ffffff, #0284c7) are used inside the message bubble styles instead of strictly adhering to design tokens.
issues.fix: Replace hex values with standard design system variable fallbacks like 'var(--ggui-color-onPrimary, #ffffff)'.

issues.description: The scrollable area lacks an aria-label identifying it as a chat log.
issues.fix: Add 'aria-live="polite"' and 'role="log"' to the scrollable Box element.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, responsive, and handles the chat logic effectively. It effectively uses the provided primitives to create a functional interface, though it misses the specific 'grouping' requirement and relies on some hardcoded colors rather than pure tokens.

issues.description: The requirement to 'group consecutive messages from same sender' was not implemented.
issues.fix: Add logic to check if message[i].sender === message[i-1].sender and hide the avatar/name metadata for consecutive messages.

issues.fix: Replace hex values with standard design system variable fallbacks like 'var(--ggui-color-onPrimary, #ffffff)'.

issues.description: The scrollable area lacks an aria-label identifying it as a chat log.
issues.fix: Add 'aria-live="polite"' and 'role="log"' to the scrollable Box element.

evaluation.critique: The component is well-structured, responsive, and handles the chat logic effectively. It effectively uses the provided primitives to create a functional interface, though it misses the specific 'grouping' requirement and relies on some hardcoded colors rather than pure tokens.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

issues.fix: Add aria-hidden='true' to the Icon component.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.fix: Add an error message or border highlight to the Email Input when the pattern doesn't match.

issues.fix: Add 'aria-label="Onboarding progress"' to the Progress component.

issues.fix: Wrap step content in a simple CSS transition or animation wrapper to provide the requested smooth transition effect.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, follows the contract perfectly, and uses the design system appropriately. It is highly functional and readable; adding subtle visual states for form validation and a basic fade animation would elevate it to a fully production-ready level.
